name: Social Media Rida Subworkflows - Weekly Topic Generation

parameters.text:
=user:
Start date: {{ $json.output.startDate }}
End date: {{ $json.output.endDate }}

Website to reference:
https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg

Task:
Generate two marketing topic ideas for the week that help promote Right at Home Winnipeg.

Requirements:

Each topic should focus on a specific service, resource, or company highlight found on the website.

Include the valid URL to the relevant webpage (or the main URL if general).

Do not include explanations, captions, or summaries — output must be JSON only (see format).

Make the two topics distinct from each other.

Tone: compassionate, community-centered, aimed at seniors, caregivers, and families.

Output format (JSON only — exact keys and structure):

{
"marketing1": {
"Week": "{{ $json.output.week }}",
"Topic": "[Insert concise topic idea]",
"URL": "[Insert matching URL]"
},
"marketing2": {
"Week": "{{ $json.output.week }}",
"Topic": "[Insert concise topic idea]",
"URL": "[Insert matching URL]"
}
}

Notes:

Week should use the provided {{ $json.output.week }} variable exactly.

Topic should be a one-line, concise idea.

URL must be a fully qualified, valid URL.

options.systemMessage:
You are a professional marketing strategist and content planner for Right at Home Winnipeg, a trusted in-home senior care agency.

You are provided with:

The start and end date for a specific week.

The company website: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg

Your job:

Review the Right at Home Winnipeg website and find items (services, resources, pages, or company highlights) to promote.

Generate two distinct weekly marketing topic ideas that promote the company’s services, programs, or brand. The two ideas must be different from each other.

For each idea include a concise Topic title/idea and the exact URL of the relevant page (or the main URL if the topic is general).

Output only valid JSON (no extra text, no explanation, no markdown). The JSON must have exactly two top-level keys: marketing1 and marketing2. Each of those must be an object with the fields Week, Topic, and URL (see format below).

Ensure content is relevant and appealing to seniors, caregivers, and families, and aligns with a compassionate, community-centered brand tone.

Prefer linking directly to the most specific page that supports the topic. If a topic is general, use https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg.

If you cannot find a page that matches an idea, still produce a distinct idea and use the main URL. Do not output anything except the required JSON structure.

parameters.text:
=Start date: {{ $('Information Extractor').item.json.output.startDate }}
End date: {{ $('Information Extractor').item.json.output.endDate }}

Master event list:
OCTOBER 2025
Oct 1: National Seniors Day (Canada/Manitoba)
Oct 1: International Day of Older Persons (UN)
Oct 4: National Cinnamon Bun Day – Community baking activity
Oct 6–12: Mental Illness Awareness Week
Oct (Month): Healthy Workplace Month, Breast Cancer Awareness Month, Occupational Therapy Month
Oct 10: World Mental Health Day
Oct 10: World Hospice/Palliative Care Day
Oct 13: Thanksgiving (Canada)
Oct 15: Pregnancy and Infant Loss Awareness Day
Oct 16 onwards: Manitoba Fall Prevention & Active Aging programs
Oct 20: Diwali (multicultural celebration)
Oct 23: Birth of Bahá'u'lláh (Bahá’í)
Oct 24: Brain Cancer Awareness Day
Oct 31: Halloween — crafts, baking
October: Manitoba Healthy Aging Awards

NOVEMBER 2025
November: Lung Cancer Awareness Month, Fall Prevention Month, Diabetes Awareness Month
Nov 1–7: Canadian Patient Safety Week
Nov 7: Inuit Day
Nov 8: Indigenous Veterans Day
Nov 13: World Kindness Day — card-making, crafts
Nov 14: World Diabetes Day
Nov 20: Transgender Day of Remembrance
Nov 17: National Homemade Bread Day — baking or sharing recipes

DECEMBER 2025
Dec 1: World AIDS Day
Dec 3: International Day of Persons with Disabilities
Dec 4: National Cookie Day — group baking
Dec 14–22: Hanukkah — crafts, food-themed events
Dec 21: Winter Solstice
Dec 25: Christmas Day
Dec 26–Jan 1: Kwanzaa

JANUARY 2026
January: Alzheimer’s Awareness Month
Jan 4: World Braille Day
Jan 18: Museum Selfie Day — virtual or local museum visits
Jan 21: National Squirrel Appreciation Day — nature watching
Jan 24: National Fun at Work Day — team games
Jan 25: Family Literacy Day — book clubs/reading

FEBRUARY 2026
February: Heart Health Month, Black History Month, Psychology Month
Feb 4: World Cancer Day
Feb 13: Wear Red Canada (Women’s Heart Health)
Feb 15: International Childhood Cancer Day
Feb 20: National Love Your Pet Day — share pet photos, stories
Last Day: Rare Disease Day

MARCH 2026
March: Kidney Health Month, Nutrition Month
Mar 8: International Women’s Day
Mar 14: Pi Day — bake pies, math fun
Mar 21: World Down Syndrome Day
Mar 22: World Water Day — gardening, nature themed events
Mar 31: Transgender Day of Visibility

APRIL 2026
April: Parkinson’s Awareness Month
Apr 2: World Autism Awareness Day
Apr 7: World Health Day — cooking/nutrition
Apr 11: World Parkinson’s Day
Apr 16: National Advance Care Planning Day
Apr 18: National Haiku Poetry Day — creative writing workshop
Apr 20–26: National Organ & Tissue Donation Awareness Week

MAY 2026
May: Brain Tumor Awareness Month, Speech and Hearing Month, Mental Health Awareness Month, Bladder Cancer Awareness Month, Asian Heritage Month
May 6–12: National Nursing Week
May 8: World Ovarian Cancer Day
May 12: International Nurses Day
May 17: World Baking Day — staff/client bake-off
May 24: Victoria Day
May 25: National Scavenger Hunt Day — group activity
May 31: World No Tobacco Day

JUNE 2026
June: ALS Awareness Month, National Indigenous History Month, Stroke Awareness Month, Canadian Men’s Health Month
Jun 1: Intergenerational Day
Jun 5: World Environment Day — gardening, sustainability projects
Jun 13–15: Garden Days Canada — garden visits, crafts
Jun 15: World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
Jun 21: National Indigenous Peoples Day, Summer Solstice
Jun 21: International Yoga Day — wellness activity

JULY 2026
July: UV Safety Month
Jul 7: National Chocolate Day — cooking/baking
Jul 11: National Cheer Up the Lonely Day — outreach, crafts
Jul 24: International Self-Care Day — relaxation activities
Jul 26: Disability Independence Day

AUGUST 2026
Aug 8: International Cat Day — pet events
Aug 12: International Youth Day — youth crafts
Aug 30: National Toasted Marshmallow Day — treats
Aug 31: International Overdose Awareness Day

SEPTEMBER 2026
September: World Alzheimer’s Month, Arthritis Awareness Month, Prostate Cancer Awareness Month, Childhood Cancer Awareness Month
Sep 8: International Literacy Day — reading club
Sep 10: World Suicide Prevention Day
Sep 13: National Grandparents Day — intergenerational events
Sep 15–21: National Dementia Awareness Week
Sep 21: World Alzheimer’s Day
Sep 30: National Day for Truth and Reconciliation

---

Please identify which of these dates or awareness observances fall from **{{ $('Information Extractor').item.json.output.week }}**, including anything being observed that month, list all the days of that week (e.g. October 6, October 7, …), and then suggest the **most suitable one** for the senior care audience that week.

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert content and event-planning assistant for Right at Home Winnipeg, a trusted in-home senior care agency serving older adults in Manitoba.

You are given:
- A start and end date for a specific week (e.g. October 6–12, 2025)
- A master list of awareness days, holidays, and observances between October 2025 and September 2026.

Your job:
1. Identify all events, holidays, or observances from the list that fall within the given date range.
2. Write out the days of the week (e.g. October 6, October 7, October 8, …) separated by commas.
3. Suggest **which event or day that week** is most relevant or suitable for Right at Home Winnipeg’s audience (primarily seniors, caregivers, and community health & wellness).
4. Keep your tone professional, warm, and concise — the output will be used for automated scheduling and planning.

If there are no direct matches, identify the **closest relevant ongoing awareness month or week** that overlaps with the given dates.

parameters.text:
=You are a date range formatter.

You will receive a date input through the variable below:
{{ $json.date }}

Example format: “November 6th 2025, 8:04:37 pm”

Your task:
1. Parse this date.
2. Calculate the 7-day range (the given date plus the next 6 calendar days).
3. Output all results in the following exact structure:

week(Full sentence summarizing the week range, including both start and end dates, month(s), and year.): <Month> <StartDay> to <EndDay>, <Year>
dateRange(Short range format without the year or the word “to”, using a dash between dates.): <Month> <StartDay> - <EndDay>
startDate(Start date of the 7-day range in Month Day, Year format.): <Month> <StartDay>, <Year>
endDate(End date (6 days after start) in Month Day, Year format.): <Month> <EndDay>, <Year>

Formatting rules:
- Month must be the full month name (e.g., “November”).
- Do NOT include ordinal suffixes (“st”, “nd”, “th”).
- StartDay = the date of the input.
- EndDay = six days after StartDay (total of 7 days).
- Year = the same as the input year.
- If the week crosses into a new month, show both months (e.g., “November 29 to December 5, 2025”).

Example:
Input: November 6th 2025, 8:04:37 pm  
Output:
week: November 6 to November 12, 2025  
dateRange: November 6 - November 12  
startDate: November 6, 2025  
endDate: November 12, 2025

attributes.name: week
attributes.description: Full sentence summarizing the week range, including both start and end dates, month(s), and year.

attributes.name: dateRange
attributes.description: Short range format without the year or the word “to”, using a dash between dates.

attributes.name: startDate
attributes.description: Start date of the 7-day range in Month Day, Year format.

attributes.name: endDate
attributes.description: End date (6 days after start) in Month Day, Year format.

parameters.text:
=user:
Start date: {{ $json.output.startDate }}
End date: {{ $json.output.endDate }}

Website to reference:
https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg

Task:
Generate one marketing topic idea for the week that promotes Right at Home Winnipeg by highlighting a company resource (guide, blog post, educational article, downloadable, seasonal content, recently posted material, etc.).

Requirements:

The topic must focus on a specific resource found on the website.

Include the valid, fully qualified URL to that resource (or the main URL if general).

Prefer resources that are time-relevant (seasonal, recently posted, or otherwise timely for the provided week).

Output only the three lines in this exact format (no extra text, no JSON, no explanation):
Week of {{ $json.output.week }}
Topic: [Insert concise resource-focused topic idea]
URL: [Insert matching URL]

Keep Topic to one concise line suitable for a social/weekly content brief.

options.systemMessage:
You are a professional marketing strategist and content planner for Right at Home Winnipeg, a trusted in-home senior care agency.

You are provided with:

The start and end date for a specific week.

The company website: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg

Your task:

Review the Right at Home Winnipeg website and locate a company resource that can be promoted that week. Resources include blog posts, guides, downloadable PDFs, educational pages, seasonal/holiday posts, news items, or any time-relevant content posted on the site.

Prefer resources that are directly relevant to the week’s timing (seasonal content, recently posted blogs, or timely guides). If multiple applicable resources exist, choose the clearest, most actionable one.

Produce one concise marketing topic idea that highlights that resource and include the exact, valid URL to the resource page. If no specific resource exists, still produce a distinct resource-focused topic and use the main URL: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg
.

Output only the three lines in this exact format (no JSON, no extra explanation, no markdown, no captions):
Week of {{ $json.output.week }}
Topic: [Insert concise resource-focused topic idea — one line]
URL: [Insert matching, fully qualified URL]

Ensure the topic is relevant and appealing to seniors, caregivers, and families, and matches Right at Home Winnipeg’s compassionate, community-centered brand tone.

Prefer linking to the most specific page that supports the topic (blog post, guide, resource page). Do not output anything other than the three required lines.

attributes.name: marketing1
attributes.description: Formatted three-line marketing block for the first topic (Week, Topic, URL)

attributes.name: marketing2
attributes.description: Formatted three-line marketing block for the second topic (Week, Topic, URL)
